title: pseuderanthemum nigritianum
intro: Pseuderanthemum nigritianum, also known as Black Eyed Susan or False Thunbergia, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Pseuderanthemum nigritianum has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. It is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion in herbal medicine, often used in tea or as a poultice to apply directly to the affected area.
